title: [apiv2] failed to create a new image from a running container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. ./bin/podman run --name myalpine -td docker.io/library/alpine:latest /bin/sh

3. curl -v -s -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"container":"myalpine","repo":"newalpine","tag":"v1","comment":"test", "author":"ajia"}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/commit

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/json|jq
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/containers/json HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Fri, 15 May 2020 10:28:25 GMT
< Content-Length: 465
<
{ [465 bytes data]
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
[
  {
    "Command": [
      "/bin/sh"
    ],
    "Created": 1589534801,
    "Exited": false,
    "ExitedAt": -62135596800,
    "ExitCode": 0,
    "Id": "88bec8231d7035f23d51380d16b3f5cf181e0d00ecbe931ab9cb7ef1716efdb7",
    "Image": "docker.io/library/alpine:latest",
    "ImageID": "f70734b6a266dcb5f44c383274821207885b549b75c8e119404917a61335981a",
    "IsInfra": false,
    "Labels": null,
    "Mounts": [],
    "Names": [
      "myalpine"
    ],
    "Namespaces": {},
    "Pid": 0,
    "Pod": "",
    "PodName": "",
    "Ports": null,
    "Size": null,
    "StartedAt": 1589534801,
    "State": "running"
  }
]

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"container":"myalpine","repo":"newalpine","tag":"v1","comment":"test", "author":"ajia"}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/li
bpod/containers/commit
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/commit HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 88
>
* upload completely sent off: 88 out of 88 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed
< Date: Fri, 15 May 2020 10:25:58 GMT
< Content-Length: 0
<
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
can successfully  create a new image from a running container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
Built:              Fri May 15 03:26:27 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
This is not a bug.  You need to pass those parameters as query parameters.  The proper syntax would be:

```'http://d/v1.40/libpod/commit?container=baude&repo=newalpine&tag=v1&comment=test&author=ajia'```

This too will then fail with an error because only comment can be used with the docker format.

Also, make sure your query parameters are http encoded as they get more complex.  if you need us to help you with that, feel free to reach out.

> 
> This too will then fail with an error because only comment can be used with the docker format.

Thanks for your comment!

It will be better if there is a comment about this in query parameters 'comment' in documentation - http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodCommitContainer

> Also, make sure your query parameters are http encoded as they get more complex. if you need us to help you with that, feel free to reach out.

It will be friendly for users to give request samples in documentation - http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodCommitContainer
Any chance you could make a PR to make these doc changes?
It seems we were missing these documentation updates
